ddls:
CREATE TABLE "covid_statistics" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each record */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the recorded statistics */,
  "infected" INTEGER /* Total number of infected individuals */,
  "exposed" INTEGER /* Total number of exposed individuals */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Total number of active cases */,
  "recovered" INTEGER /* Total number of recovered cases */,
  "deaths" INTEGER /* Total number of deaths */,
  "diff_exposed" INTEGER /* Change in exposed cases compared to the previous day */,
  "diff_active" INTEGER /* Change in active cases compared to the previous day */,
  "diff_recovered" INTEGER /* Change in recovered cases compared to the previous day */,
  "diff_deaths" INTEGER /* Change in deaths compared to the previous day */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data source */,
  "hospitalized" INTEGER /* Number of individuals hospitalized */,
  "icu" INTEGER /* Number of individuals in ICU */,
  "vaccinated" INTEGER /* Number of vaccinated individuals */,
  "test_conducted" INTEGER /* Number of tests conducted */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_covid_statistics_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_covid_statistics_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the region is located */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the region */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographical latitude */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographical longitude */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, public health official) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "website" TEXT /* Website for data source */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the data source */,
  "update_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of data updates (e.g., daily, weekly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "date_sent" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "read_status" INTEGER /* Whether the notification has been read */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., researcher, public health official) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* List of permissions associated with the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "region_statistics" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each record */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the recorded statistics */,
  "infected" INTEGER /* Total number of infected individuals for the region */,
  "exposed" INTEGER /* Total number of exposed individuals for the region */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Total number of active cases for the region */,
  "recovered" INTEGER /* Total number of recovered cases for the region */,
  "deaths" INTEGER /* Total number of deaths for the region */,
  "hospitalized" INTEGER /* Number of hospitalized individuals in the region */,
  "icu" INTEGER /* Number of individuals in ICU in the region */,
  "vaccinated" INTEGER /* Number of vaccinated individuals in the region */,
  "test_conducted" INTEGER /* Number of tests conducted in the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_region_statistics_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)
